purpose: This policy describes how <<LAB_NAME>> runs QC for CLIA-waived tests. Although CLIA does not specify QC frequency for waived tests, the lab follows the manufacturer's QC instructions per 42 CFR 493.15(e), and tracks QC results to detect drift.

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Laboratories performing waived tests
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: Laboratories must follow manufacturers' test instructions and meet the requirements specified in 493.35, 493.37, 493.39, 493.43, 493.45, and 493.51, when applicable, and the conditions specified in 493.5, 493.17, and 493.19.

scope: All <<LAB_NAME>> personnel who perform waived testing and the medical director or designee.
policy_statements: QC for every waived test is run at the manufacturer's specified frequency at minimum; the lab may run additional QC if recommended by the medical director or designee for the lab's risk profile.
QC materials are stored, handled, and used per the manufacturer's instructions; expired QC material is not used.
QC results are documented with: control identity, lot number, expiration, result, accept/reject decision, operator, date/time.
Failed QC triggers immediate corrective action: investigate (operator, device, lot, environment), document, rerun QC; patient testing pauses on the affected device until QC is in control.
QC results are reviewed monthly by the medical director or designee; trends are analyzed and corrective action taken where indicated.
QC records are retained for at least 2 years per 42 CFR 493.1105.
procedure_steps: At the manufacturer-specified frequency, the operator runs the required QC on the device.
QC result is interpreted against the manufacturer's acceptance criteria.
If accepted, the operator documents the QC (control, lot, result, operator, date/time) and proceeds with patient testing.
If rejected, patient testing pauses; the operator investigates (operator technique, device issue, expired control, environmental factor), rerunds QC.
If QC cannot be brought into control, the device is taken out of service; the medical director or designee is notified; affected prior patient results are reviewed for potential impact.
The medical director or designee reviews QC documentation monthly and signs the review.
definitions: Quality control performed per the test manufacturer's instructions on a CLIA-waived test. CLIA does not specify frequency; manufacturer instructions control.
QC using a control material separate from the patient sample. Most waived tests have external QC requirements at minimum per lot, per shipment, or per operator.
Built-in QC features of the device itself (e.g., internal control line on a lateral flow assay). Manufacturer-defined; documented per device.
